text:
Testing plays a crucial role in software development. Unit tests check individual parts of the code in isolation. Integration tests ensure that these parts work together correctly. Together, they help catch bugs early.

One popular approach is test-driven development. In this method, you write the test first, then the code to pass it, and finally refactor. This process encourages cleaner code and reduces mistakes.

Above all, thorough testing gives teams confidence. When a project has comprehensive tests, developers can add new features or make changes without fear of breaking existing functionality. This makes the software more reliable and easier to maintain over time.
